description: Cost of stepping from biome A to biome B during macro-level path finding. Lower = more thematically / geologically adjacent. Self-distance is 0; 1 is for biome *pairs so close the same step can be in both* (there's no room authored to bridge them); 2 is adjacent but with a visible seam (a bridged pair â€” a transition room is authored); 3 is distant; 4 is opposite extremes. The cost > 1 threshold drives transition-room insertion, so the 8 authored transition pairs (forestâ†”swamp, swampâ†”ruin, ruinâ†”tomb, urbanâ†”ruin, mountainâ†”cavern, cavernâ†”tomb, desertâ†”ruin, forestâ†”mountain) are all cost 2 â€” the seam is real enough that a bridging room reads naturally, but adjacency sampling still prefers these over cost-3 or cost-4 jumps. swampâ†”desert (moisture extreme) is cost 4. The matrix is symmetric by construction.
